pattern: Fixing a pair of columns reduces the rectangle to a one-dimensional problem of finding the largest subarray sum not exceeding k, which an ordered set of running prefix sums answers in logarithmic time per element.
hints: Every candidate rectangle spans some contiguous range of columns, so fix the left and right column bounds and collapse each row in between into a single value.
With the columns fixed you have a 1D array; you want the maximum subarray sum that stays at or below k, which is a question about pairs of prefix sums.

Maintain the running prefix sums seen so far in a sorted structure; for the current prefix S, the best subarray ending here that is at most k comes from the smallest earlier prefix that is at least S - k.

edge_inputs.description: small matrix where the best rectangle equals k exactly
single row, whole row is the best at or below k
k smaller than every single positive cell forces a negative-sum rectangle
single cell matrix, the only rectangle is that cell
k larger than the total sum so the global maximum rectangle wins
single column forces vertical rectangles only
all negative cells, best is the largest single value at or below k

stress.description: small dense matrix with mixed signs and a moderate k so the quadratic oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 8)
    n = rng.randint(1, 8)
    mat = [[rng.randint(-20, 20) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    total = sum(sum(r) for r in mat)
    k = rng.randint(-30, max(0, total) + 5)
    return [mat, k]
stress.note: matrix dimensions capped at 8x8 so the O((mn)^2) brute oracle is quick and a feasible rectangle always exists
